entry.title: Re: How to configure Single Sign-On (SSO) between S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi Khaing, for your SSO requirement between S/4HANA Cloud and BTP, I suggest exploring SAP Build Work Zone. It serves as a centralized entry point that can handle authentication seamlessly across both environments. 

Search for "SAP Build Work Zone" on developers.sap.com -  you'll find plenty of tutorials covering integration and SSO setup (specifically principal propagation) between S/4HANA and BTP. Good luck!

entry.title: Re: Ajuste de inventário com emissão de Nota Fiscal
entry.content: Bom dia! Sei que a thread tem mais de 10 anos, mas me deparei com este cenário recentemente em um projeto e estou com algumas dúvidas. No caso é possível gerar a nota automaticamente de forma standard após o uso da transação MI07 e a geração do documento de material? Quando realizo esse procedimento é informado o erro 8B262 - Nenhuma nota fiscal ligada ao documento. Realizei as configurações derivação do tipo de nota de nota fiscal na visão J_1BIM02V, mas ainda sim, a nota fiscal não é gerada. Se possível, poderia esclarecer o caso por favor?

entry.title: What’s New in Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 release delivers a strong collection of enhancements across Asset Management, with improvements in maintenance execution, safety, planning, technical object management, and extensibility.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (the foundation of SAP Cloud ERP Private) strengthens efficient, data‑driven maintenance in this release while elevating user experience and process consistency across the entire asset lifecycle.

In this blog post, you’ll find a curated overview of the most important innovations, grouped by theme and written for maintenance technicians, planners, reliability engineers, and integration partners who rely on SAP to keep operations running safely and efficiently.

For a broader look at what’s new across other lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS01 release, don’t miss the blog by Yannick Peterschmitt: What’s New in the SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 FPS1

Table of content:

  • Safety, compliance & field execution excellence
    - Isolation Management in Permit to Work – Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) 
    - Associated work permits in Manage Maintenance Orders app 
    - Inspection checklist enhancements 
    - After‑job recording for unplanned work 
    - New capabilities in Perform Maintenance Jobs app
  • Maintenance request & work pack enhancements
    - Improvements in Screen Maintenance Requests app 
    - Custom logic to add e‑mail recipients for job pack outputs
  • Planning and strategy enhancements
    - Additional functionality in Manage Maintenance Plans and Items apps 
    - Extended planning bucket capabilities 
    - Improvements for phase control code and analytics based on phases
  • Master data and technical object innovations
    - Reuse value help app for technical objects 
    - Hierarchy structure view for reference structures

  • SAP Asset Performance Management 
  • More information

Safety, compliance & field execution excellence

Isolation Management in Permit to Work – Lockout/Tagout (LOTO)

The 2025 FPS01 release introduces a fully embedded Isolation Management capability that brings the complete Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) lifecycle into SAP S/4HANA Asset Management. Through new configuration options and dedicated Fiori apps, maintenance organizations can structure and govern how isolation activities are defined, prepared, approved, and executed.

A new customizing section enables planning plants to specify isolation certificate behavior, certificate and template types, number ranges, and approval setups. The Manage Isolation Certificate Templates app (F8505) allows users to build reusable templates by adding technical objects, attaching work permit templates, assigning approvers, and adding supporting documents, ensuring consistent and compliant LOTO preparation.

Manage Isolation Certificate Templates app (F8505)

 

With the Manage Isolation Certificates app (F8504), users can create certificates from scratch or from a predefined template, add and process isolation items, move them through operational cycles such as tagging, testing, and untagging, assign work permits and approvals, and attach relevant documents. Printable outputs for certificates, lists, and tags support on‑site execution.

These innovations strengthen safety compliance and reduce operational risk through modernized digital isolation management, improves coordination between operations and maintenance to boost asset productivity, enhances the handling of technical objects during LOTO procedures, and increases overall usability of the permit‑to‑work process.

Check this Road Map item: Isolation management in permit to work (lockout tagout)

Associated work permits in Manage Maintenance Orders app

Work permits are now seamlessly integrated into the Manage Maintenance Orders (F4604) app, becoming available whenever a Permit‑to‑Work profile is maintained for the planning plant. A dedicated Work Permits section provides immediate visibility of permit details, including approval status, implemented precautions, certificate progress, and handout information.

Users can navigate directly to the related permit or create a new one linked to the order and planning plant. In addition, a Fiori‑based mass creation job enables work permits to be generated for multiple orders at once, using filtering criteria and template determination to streamline preparation and ensure consistent alignment with Work Clearance Management.

Read more about it: Mass Generation of Permit to Work Objects | SAP Help Portal

Work permits section in Manage Maintenance Orders (F4604) app

 

Inspection checklist enhancements

Inspection checklist processing receives several refinements that improve configuration, transparency, and usability across maintenance workflows. A new customizing knot centralizes all checklist‑related settings, creating a clearer foundation for managing current and future checklist configurations. Updated output forms now reflect material‑based checklists, covering serial‑number and component information in maintenance orders and work packs, ensuring documents accurately represent checklist content.

Enhancements in the Manage Inspection Checklists app (F6607) introduce richer detail in inspection lots, including quantities, reservation data, component references, serial numbers (if enabled), and new filtering options such as technical object and inspection lot status. Task lists now display a material checklist indicator, and the related API has been expanded to support reading, creating, and updating this field.

Within Manage Maintenance Orders app (F5241), checklist types are visible at operation level, cross‑app navigation to inspection checklists is supported, and actions such as “Generate Checklist” streamline preparation. Authorization checks for checklist types help ensure only permitted users can assign or modify certain checklist categories, providing stronger control over sensitive checklist usage.

Find more in the Road Map: SAP Road Map Explorer

Manage Inspection Checklists and Manage Maintenance Orders apps

After‑job recording for unplanned work

Technicians can now document maintenance activities that were performed immediately during an ad‑hoc repair, even when no prior order or confirmation existed. Using the new Confirmation of Unplanned Work processing context, they record failure data, measurements, consumed components, attachments, and time confirmations after completing the physical repair, ensuring proper traceability without delaying the actual fix.

A dedicated customizing knot controls default behaviors such as notification type, priority, and order type, and determines whether statuses like Work Completed or Job Finished are automatically applied. Authorization checks ensure only permitted users can create or submit unplanned work confirmations. Technicians can start the process either from the Perform Maintenance Jobs (F5104A) app, where the context is preselected, or from Create Maintenance Request (F1511A), where it must be chosen manually.

Once submitted, the system creates the necessary order operation (often directly in the MAIN execution stage) and enables technicians to capture all required details in a structured flow. Additional behavior, such as phase transitions or automatic technical completion, follows plant‑specific customizing settings.

Check more in the Road Map: SAP Road Map Explorer

Confirmation of Unplanned Work

 

 

New capabilities in Perform Maintenance Jobs app

The Perform Maintenance Jobs app (F5104A) receives numerous usability and functional enhancements that make job execution, time recording, and material handling more transparent and efficient. Key improvements include:

Configuration & time recording

  • New customizing knot for clearer setup of all F5104A‑related configuration steps.
  • Flexible defaulting of employee for time confirmations (logged‑in user, main technician, previous confirmation, or empty).
  • Introduction of Degree of Progress to capture physical work completion independently from actual work time, with UI slider and defaults based on last confirmation.

Perform Maintenance Jobs app (F5104A)

Job pausing & transparency

  • New pause codes for documenting why a job is paused, with optional or mandatory entry modes and full history tracking.

Notification processing

  • Long text templates can be applied during malfunction data capture, supporting guided, structured input and respecting notification type text rules.

Technical object visibility

  • New overview of object list entries with processing‑indicator editing (if authorized) and cross‑app navigation.

Material handling improvements

  • Transparency for EWM‑managed materials, automatically disabling Post/Return actions when required.
  • Better handling for batch‑managed materials, including mandatory batch entry and smart‑link navigation from material numbers.
  • Enhanced support for serialized materials, including value help and duplicate‑number checks during posting.

List page enhancements

  • A redesigned list table with more visible results, improved column management, context menu, and richer notification quick info.

Redesigned list table in Perform Maintenance Jobs app

Maintenance request & work pack enhancements

Improvements in Screen Maintenance Requests app

The Screen Maintenance Requests (F4072) Fiori app now adopts a flexible column layout so users can view the request list alongside its details in resizable, side‑by‑side columns, reducing back‑and‑forth navigation and accelerating review.

In addition, a new Change Scheduling action enables mass updates of open notifications for key scheduling fields (Priority, Maintenance Event, Required Start, Required Finish, and Final Due Date) directly from the list page; changes are captured in the History tab for traceability of updates to consequence and likelihood values.

Screen Maintenance Requests (F4072) app

 

Custom logic to add e‑mail recipients for job pack outputs

A new enhancement makes it easier to distribute job pack and work pack outputs to all relevant stakeholders. Using a dedicated BAdI, planners can now add additional e‑mail recipients to work pack output items, such as pick lists or instructions, through Output Parameter Determination. This logic can be activated for specific output types and e‑mail channels (To, Cc, Bcc), and supports flexible recipient management via custom tables, reusable elements, or SAP distribution lists.

At the same time, work pack creation becomes more versatile. The previous limitation restricting creation to execution or post‑execution phases is removed; planners can now enable work pack creation across the entire maintenance order lifecycle based on work pack type settings. New customizing options allow organizations to manage where restrictions apply for both phase and non‑phase‑enabled orders, increasing control and flexibility.

Custom logic to add e‑mail recipients for job pack outputs

Planning and strategy enhancements

Additional functionality in Manage Maintenance Plans and Items apps

Maintenance planners gain more flexibility and control through several usability improvements in the Manage Maintenance Plan (F5325) and Manage Maintenance Item (F5356) apps. The plan app now allows users to search maintenance plans directly by description, making it easier to identify and retrieve relevant plans.

In the maintenance item app, planners can filter items by maintenance plan status and display the plan status directly in the item list, offering clearer visibility into which items belong to which plan stage.

Manage Maintenance Plan (F5325) and Manage Maintenance Item (F5356) apps

Extended planning bucket capabilities

The planning bucket framework is expanded to give maintenance planners more control over how backlogs are grouped and prepared for execution. With this enhancement, users can define a broader and more flexible range of maintenance backlogs to be included in a reference or event-based planning bucket. This includes the ability to add additional grouping fields, enabling maintenance teams to organize work in ways that reflect real operational structures or business priorities.

For example, planners can now link an improvement project to a planning bucket, allowing all backlogs associated with that project to be bundled into a single, coherent scope. This creates a more holistic view of the work that needs to be scheduled and makes it easier to consolidate activities that naturally belong together.

These enhancements optimize the scope of maintenance backlogs planned within a given period, while giving planners the flexibility to reprioritize and adjust the bucket contents based on available capacity, budget, and strategic initiatives.

Improvements for phase control code and analytics based on phases

This enhancement strengthens the way maintenance organizations use the phase‑based maintenance model by improving both the usability of phase control codes and the analytics available around phase progression.

The definition of phase control codes has been simplified, so the same codes can be reused across different maintenance‑order types. This ensures more consistent behavior across teams and scenarios. The translation of both the phase control code and its description have been streamlined as well, making the codes easier to understand and apply in multi‑language environments.

In addition, new analytics allow planners to analyze the throughput of backlogs based on the average time each backlog item takes to move through the maintenance phases. This enables better visibility of bottlenecks and helps identify root causes behind slowdowns or delays. The result is clearer insights into phase progression and improved control of the maintenance workflow. 

Phase control code and analytics based on phases

 

Master data and technical object innovations

Reuse value help app for technical objects

Finding the right technical object, whether equipment or a functional location, often meant navigating different value help dialogs with varying layouts and search behaviours. To simplify this experience, a new unified value help has been introduced across Asset Management. This enhancement provides a single, consistent, and intuitive way to search for equipment, functional locations, and other technical objects in Fiori apps.

The new Technical Object Reuse Value Help is built on RAP‑based CDS views and delivers a more efficient, flexible search. Users can choose from multiple search templates, each surfacing different fields depending on their task, for example, focusing on labels, maintenance data, sales information, or address details.

This not only speeds up how users find the right object but also brings a more modern and consistent user experience across apps such as Find Technical Object (F2072) Manage Technical Object BOMs (F6070) and Manage Serial Numbers (F7617).

Behind the scenes, the value help objects for equipment, technical objects, and functional locations are fully reusable, so any Fiori app can adopt them without redesign, ensuring a harmonized look and feel across the maintenance process.

Technical Object Reuse Value Help

 

Hierarchy structure view for reference structures

Managing consistent and scalable asset structures becomes easier with two major innovations: Reference Equipment and Reference Structures. Together, they introduce a more controlled and harmonized approach to defining master data structures across plants and sites.

Reference equipment serves as a master template for creating and governing individual equipment records. It centralizes key information, such as general data, classification, measuring points, partner data, and change history, and provides a consistent basis for downstream equipment creation and updates through the Manage Reference Equipment app (F8304).

Reference structures provide a hierarchical view of reference functional locations and reference equipment, displayed in an intuitive split‑screen layout that combines structure navigation with detailed object information. Using the Manage Reference Structures apps (F8311/F8669), organizations can build and maintain standard structural templates and use them as the master for mass creation of individual structures.

Together, these capabilities help enforce consistent asset modeling across sites, enable mass changes, and reduce manual effort and errors.

Manage Reference Equipment app (F8304)

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management integrates with SAP Cloud ERP Private and empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. The 2602 release includes improvements such as smarter notification replication between SAP ERP and APM, expanded industrial system modeling, extensible and time-aware FMEA assessments, and streamlined rule execution. Together, these updates help reduce manual effort while improving data clarity, structural transparency, and lifecycle control. Read the complete blog post by Kate Dowle: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2602.

If you’d like to stay up to date with the latest APM innovations, follow my colleague Kate Dowle who regularly shares updates and new blog posts on the topic.

Summary

With these delivered innovations, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition FPS01 significantly strengthens Asset Management capabilities, from daily technician execution to long‑term planning and technical object governance. Each enhancement contributes to safer operations, improved data consistency, and more efficient maintenance processes, supporting organizations on their path toward intelligent, connected, and resilient asset performance.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Also, visit the Asset Management community page. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to find our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

More information

  • What’s New Viewer
  • From Innovation to Impact: SAP Cloud ERP Private 2025 Release (SAP News Center)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Community
  • Help Portal SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • What's New for Joule Capabilities
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • SAP Business Suite Product Expert Training H1 2026

We at Cloud ERP Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: ‌🇩🇪‌ DSAG Jahreskongress 2026 (German SAP User Group Annual Conference)
entry.content:
We are inviting you to visit the DSAG Jahreskongress 2026, the leading annual conference for SAP user companies in German‑speaking countries. The event will take place in Cologne.

The DSAG annual congress is widely regarded as the most important meeting point for SAP customers in the region. It provides a structured setting for professional exchange, practical orientation, and networking among SAP users, partners, and SAP representatives.

Participants can expect a comprehensive program covering current developments in and around SAP. The agenda combines customer experience reports, expert contributions, and opportunities for direct dialogue, supporting informed discussion on both operational and strategic topics.

Localization topics play a key role throughout the event. This year, SAP Globalization will focus on the following areas:

  • e‑Invoicing
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Payroll Germany
More information is here.

For detailed information and updates on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in Germany, join the Germany – SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Work Zone group.

Sales order from headerquarter> MRP generates PR> Convert to PO> Goods receipts> Produce finished goods> Delivery to headquarter .

If you know, please give me some idea.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Message no. VT806 The cost center is not defined
entry.content:
Hello @alex9199 

Even though the cost center exists in the hierarchy, SAP throws VT806 because:
-the cost center belongs to a different controlling area, or
-the validity period does not match, or
-the company code controlling area assignment is inconsistent.

Quick Troubleshooting-

Check KS03 → Controlling Area of the cost center
Verify valid-from / valid-to dates
Confirm company code ↔ controlling area assignment
Ensure the cost center allows postings

 

Situation / QuestionExplanation, Checks & SAP Notes
Error VT806 appears when assigning a cost centerThis error usually occurs when the system cannot validate the cost center against the controlling area or validity period used in SD configuration. Even if the cost center exists in the hierarchy, it must match the controlling parameters used during the assignment.
Cost center exists in standard hierarchyBeing in the hierarchy alone is not sufficient. The cost center must also be valid for the posting date and assigned to the correct controlling area used by the company code in SD configuration.
Most common causeThe cost center belongs to a different controlling area than the company code used in the SD determination setup.
Validity date issueIf the cost center validity period does not cover the document or configuration date, SAP treats it as undefined. Check validity start/end dates in the cost center master data.
Cost center category restrictionsSome SD processes check cost center categories or require the cost center to allow postings from the SD account assignment category.
Incorrect organizational assignmentEnsure the cost center is assigned to the correct company code / controlling area relationship in controlling settings.
Transport / client mismatchSometimes the cost center exists in development but not in the client where configuration is being performed.
Tables involvedCost center master data stored in CSKS / CSKT. Controlling area assignment stored in TKA01 / TKA02.
How to verify1️⃣ Check cost center in KS03 2️⃣ Verify controlling area and validity dates 3️⃣ Confirm company code–controlling area assignment 4️⃣ Retry assignment in SD configuration.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Cost Center Master Data in Controlling SAP Help → Account Assignment in Sales and Distribution
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 1156325 – Cost Center Validation in SD Account Assignment 📌 SAP Note 1706300 – Cost Center Not Defined Error Analysis 📌 SAP Note 2220152 – Organizational Assignments in S/4HANA


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: How to enable rule valuation group code for transacti...
entry.content:
Hello @leah_huang71 ,

The plant does not have a valuation grouping code assigned, so SAP searches OBYC using a blank grouping code instead of the rule configured for your specific grouping code.

Recommended Fix

Go to OMWD
Assign the correct Valuation Grouping Code to the plant
Ensure OBYC (FR3) entry exists for that grouping code
Test posting again

Best practice is to assign valuation grouping codes consistently across plants to simplify OBYC configuration.

 

Scenario / QuestionExplanation, Root Cause & SAP Notes
Rule based on Valuation Group Code configured in OBYC for FR3In OBYC, account determination can depend on Valuation Group Code (BWMOD) which groups plants to use the same G/L account determination logic.
Issue occurs only for plants without valuation modificationIf a plant does not have a valuation modification assigned, SAP treats the plant as blank valuation grouping code during account determination. Therefore, the OBYC rule expecting a specific valuation group code will not be triggered.
Why configuration appears not to workOBYC searches account determination keys using the following hierarchy: 1️⃣ Chart of accounts 2️⃣ Transaction key (FR3) 3️⃣ Valuation grouping code 4️⃣ Valuation class If the plant has no valuation grouping code, the system looks for a blank entry in OBYC instead.
Where valuation grouping code is definedConfiguration path: OMWD – Assign Valuation Grouping Code to Valuation Areas (Plants)
If valuation grouping code is missingThe system uses plant-specific valuation area logic, and OBYC entries that depend on grouping code will not match.
Correct configuration approachEither: ✔ Assign the same valuation grouping code to the plant in OMWD, or ✔ Maintain OBYC account determination for blank valuation grouping code.
Why many companies use valuation groupingIt reduces OBYC entries by allowing multiple plants to share the same account determination rules.
Tables involved• T001K – Valuation area configuration • T030 – Automatic account determination (OBYC) • T001W – Plant master data
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Automatic Account Determination in Materials Management SAP Help → Valuation Grouping Code Concept
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 307648 – Valuation Grouping Code in Automatic Account Determination 📌 SAP Note 157804 – OBYC Account Determination Logic 📌 SAP Note 1916010 – Account Determination Issues with Valuation Areas

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: BW/4HANA - how to find fast growing tables
entry.content:
Hi

This depends on system release ... tipically you can use  transaction DB02 on BW on hana or transaction DBACOCKPIT in BW4 (alternatevelyDB02OLD)

space-->tables-->top growers

Put particular attention to CHANGE LOGS TABLES  ( if not managed these can grow very fast)

/BIC/A<NAME OF DSO>C

Change logs tables should be erased every 10 days with automatic processes in process Chains. Many SAP notes are available on this topic depending on different system releases 

hope it helps

entry.title: Як прискорити перехід на SAP S/4HANA? Практичний інтенсив по Integrated Toolchain 🚀
entry.content:
Трансформація системи — це не лише технічне завдання, а стратегічний крок для бізнесу. Щоб зробити цей процес прозорим та керованим, SAP пропонує набір інтегрованих інструментів.

Запрошуємо на Hands-on Bootcamp: Leveraging the Integrated Toolchain for System Conversion! 🛠️

Про що ви дізнаєтесь? Ця сесія в реальному часі фокусується на тому, як поєднати методологію та інструментарій для успішної конверсії системи. Ми розберемо роль ключових компонентів:

🔹 SAP Signavio — для аналізу та оптимізації бізнес-процесів.

🔹 SAP LeanIX — для керування архітектурою та прозорості ІТ-ландшафту.

🔹 SAP Cloud ALM — для управління життєвим циклом рішення та координації впровадження.

Чому варто відвідати?

✅ Hands-on досвід: це не просто теорія, а практичні вправи.

✅ Наскрізний сценарій: ви побачите, як інструменти взаємодіють між собою на кожному етапі проєкту.

✅ Clean Core: дізнаєтесь, як підтримувати "чисте ядро" під час трансформації.

🔗Реєструйтеся за посиланням!

entry.title: Introducing CQC Implementation Gates for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
I´m  excited to introduce the continuous quality check (CQC) Implementation Gates for SAP SuccessFactors HCM. This is a remote service included in your SAP SuccessFactors subscription through SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition, at no extra cost.

Leverage SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors is essential to achieve a successful SAP SuccessFactors implementation. The service provides guidelines and recommended actions for remediation, confirming that projects meet both compliance and project management standards by verifying key deliverables at the end of a phase before progressing to the next.

The CQC for implementation gates keeps your project on track by verifying key deliverables at the end of each SAP Activate phase and serves as a checklist-based milestone to ensure compliance with SAP best practices and the SAP Activate methodology.

Key features:
  • The goal of this service is to ensure project alignment with SAP best practices and methodology standards by verifying key deliverables at the end of a phase before progressing to the next.
Benefits of this service:
  • Ensure project alignment by adhering to SAP best practices and the SAP Activate methodology, fostering consistent quality.
  • Maintain high standards by verifying key deliverables at each phase for quality assurance.
  • Build stakeholder confidence by demonstrating compliance and adherence to best practices throughout the project lifecycle.
Service can be used:
  • At the end of each SAP Activate phase. Currently, the CQC service is only available for the Prepare and Explore phases.
  • The CQC for Realize, Deploy, and Run phases are expected later in 2026.
Service cannot be used: 

If you require:

  • A project knowledge assessment.
  • A review of project deliverables (for example, project plan or configuration backlog list).
  • SAP professional services involvement and checks on traditional project management activities

Please check the CQC Implementation Gates for SAP SuccessFactors HCM infosheet in SAP Support Portal Page to learn about the key features and benefits of this service, when to use it (or not) and further details of the delivery.

How to request these remote services?

Remote Services can be requested via the Get Support application in SAP for Me (SAP Note 1296527). When submitting your request, please select:

  • Product: “Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services”
  • Product function: “Service request for a Cloud Solution”

If you need assistance in submitting your case, you may contact your local Customer Interaction Center (CIC).

Please note the priority of these cases cannot be high or very high. This product and product function do not accept these priorities.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps 

Beyond this CQC, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription.

Visit us on SAP Community for more information and to get access. Don’t forget you can “Request a Call” with one of our topic experts to connect you with the right enablement resources, fast.

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 

About the author: 

  • Alejandro Azcarate is a Global Topic Owner for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in SAP SuccessFactors HCM. Based in Madrid, Spain, Alejandro has been with SAP since 2007, holding various roles across the organization. Today, he focuses on helping businesses navigate HR transformation through SAP Enterprise Support offerings. 

💡 Have questions or feedback on this content? Don't hesitate to comment down below, contact @alejandroazca or submit them in the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps Q&A section.

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Update March 2026

Our first release in 2026 introduces substantial updates and new features for the Partner Reference Application “Poetry Slam Manager".

First of all we adopted new SAP Build service plans "build-default" and "build-runtime" for SAP Business Application Studio, CI/CD, Cloud Foundry Runtime, and SAP Build Work Zone. With this step the reference example can be developed and tested using free partner BTP licenses for test, demo, and development. 

Following your requests and feedback we extended the poetry slam manager by an example demonstrating how to integrate the SAP Document Management Service for storing attachments.

The chapter "Enable Business Logic Extensibility" paves the road for customer tenant specific extensions including coded business logic. Stay tuned for more details about customer-specific extensions for multitenant solutions!

To align with enterprise standards for embedded AI use cases, we have enhanced the code examples and tutorials, providing clearer guidance on integrating the SAP AI Core service through the AI orchestration workflow, along with practical insights into AI ethics and SAP best practices.

New chapters about SAP Cloud Logging sizing and SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery help to optimize operations and cost estimations.

We also demonstrate how to use SAP Build Work Zone together with the integrated SAP Alert Notification Service to trigger in-place notifications within your Work Zone business site and deliver them via email. In addition, dynamic tiles displaying key performance indicators for web applications provide a well-rounded finishing touch to the solution launchpad.

And last, but not least, we complemented the repository with Bruno-collections that showcase example API requests using the multitenant solution’s OData APIs in remote integration scenarios.

entry.title: Celebrating the SAP Campus Ambassadors Community
entry.content:
On March 4th, our SAP Campus Ambassadors community came together to celebrate something that makes this program so special: the people behind it.

Colleagues and ambassadors gathered to connect, exchange ideas and take a moment to recognize the impact this community continues to create. It was a chance to step away from day-to-day activities, meet fellow ambassadors in person and celebrate the shared mission that brings us together.

What made this celebration even more special is that we were part of something much bigger. The SAP Campus Ambassadors Celebration took place simultaneously in 14 locations around the world, alongside the main event in Walldorf, Germany. Being part of this global moment reminded us how large and diverse this community truly is  and how much there is to learn from one another across regions 💙

The event created a space not only to celebrate achievements, but also to reconnect as a community. Conversations sparked new ideas, experiences were shared and many of us left feeling even more inspired about the future of the program.

The SAP Campus Ambassadors program plays an important role in building bridges between SAP and universities, helping students explore careers in technology while strengthening the connection between academia and industry. But what truly brings this initiative to life is the enthusiasm and dedication of our ambassadors.

Seeing the energy, curiosity and commitment within this community is always a powerful reminder of why this program matters.

As we look ahead, we are excited to continue growing the SAP Campus Ambassadors program throughout 2026, creating new opportunities for collaboration, learning and innovation together.

To everyone who joined the celebration — thank you for your engagement, your ideas and the energy you bring to this community.

Take a look at some highlights from some of  the on-site events here:

entry.title: How to add pricing conditions dynamically in custom form template for sales documents
entry.content:
Hello,

I've created a copy of standard sales order template, the data source is FDP_V1_ORDER_CONFIRM_SRV.
I'm using $.SalesOrderNode.PriceTotalConditions table in my template, but I'd like to know how can I dynamically add items for this table. For example, if a sales order has 5 conditions, then 5 conditions to be printed, if other sales order has 2 conditions then 2 condition should be printed.

How can I proceed?

Kind regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add pricing conditions dynamically in custom f...
entry.content:
Hello.

For the conditions table to grow dynamically according to the conditions quantity, you need to check the "Repeat Table for Each Data Item" indicator. This indicator can be found in the 'Binding' section of the table. You can also define the minimum and maximum of items for this table.

Note: remember to, on the pricing procedure, adjust accordingly the 'Print Type' attribute for each pricing condition. Make sure to check out this blog: Extending Form Templates for Sales Document Output | SAP Community

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
Hi @edina_j0zs4 

Thank you very much for the explanation and for sharing the document. It is an excellent document.

I would like to ask if it is possible to redetermine the Check Rule associated with the Business Partner during the credit check process. To provide some context: using ECC, and for the same customer, depending on the Credit Control Area, they assign different risk classes, each with different verification parameters.

When trying to replicate this behavior in FSCM, we noticed that the Check Rule is not defined per credit segment, but rather at a general level within the credit profile. This makes it difficult to handle different verification parameters for the same customer depending on the segment.

For this reason, I would like to know if there is a way to redetermine the Check Rule at runtime during the credit check, so that different verification parameters can be considered depending on the credit segment involved in the verification. I was considering whether this could be implemented using a BAdI at the start of the credit check process, or if there is any standard alternative in FSCM that would allow achieving behavior similar to what existed in ECC.

I would greatly appreciate any guidance you could provide on the best way to approach this scenario.

Best regards.

entry.title: How can I get data field "content" in PPD

I want to automate the proces of filling up pallets. At a resource there is a resource bin which should contain an empty box. During production we fill this box with products and when its full we trigger a logistic request. To do this Ive configured a box material called SEATTUBE_EXTRUSION_CUT_PALLET of type Packaging. I created a Data type with 1 field for MAX quantity and added this to the material:

 

When loading the resource bin with a component I can select this material and fill the data type field with a number.

Unfortunately I cannot find any way of retrieving this data. In the retrieve resources this data does not show up:

The resource bins are not available in the MDO's nor are the data field "contents" available in any API. 

We cannot use the maxQuantity field of the resource bin since every packaging placed there can hold different maximum quantities. 

How can I achieve this?

entry.title: It is possible to deploy two CAP Apps to the same PostgreSQL database in Cloud Foundry?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Me and my colleagues are new to CAP development, we are using tutorials and the documentation as source for our first steps. We were able already to create and deploy one CAP App on Cloud Foundry, connected to one PosgtreSQL Instance, which is working like a charm.

However now we need to build a second app, which has a different schema.cds file. We built it, tested it locally (in memory database), but when we try to deploy it, we always receive one error related to a DROP TABLE command.

[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] 09...e [2026-03-04T15:47:40.269285] /> deployment to postgres-3...ws.com:8501 failed (STDOUT, APP/TASK/deploy-to-postgresql)#
[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] 09...ee [2026-03-04T15:47:40.270089] Error: CDS compilation failed (@sap/cds-compiler v6.7.3) (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-to-postgresql)#
#2026 03 04 15:47:44.785#Z#DEBUG#43...-postgres-deployer#flowable-async-job-executor-thread-33947#
[PollExecuteTaskStatusExecution] 093...e [2026-03-04T15:47:40.270114] Error: Dropping tables is not supported (in artifact:“db.BusinessCaseHeaderConfig”) (STDERR, APP/TASK/deploy-to-postgresql)#

 

The db.BusinessCaseHeaderConfig is actually one table of our other app.

As a workaround for this, we added at the mta.yaml, at the cap service configuration the following PG configuration:

    properties:
      PGOPTIONS: -c search_path=workflowconfig,public
 
In this way, we force our app to use a different schema, which we had to create manually at the PostgreSQL Instance.
 
I would like to know if is there any other way to do the deployment of two CAP Apps, with different schemas into the same PostgreSQL and schema?
Does anyone has experience with that? Is that even possible?

We saw that the content of the table cds_model is what contain the model information, we think that here is the problem, this table only accepts one line, therefore only one app.

BR
Eduardo

entry.title: SAP’s Most Popular Certification Gets a Major Upgrade
entry.content:
March is officially the month of ABAP. SAP’s most popular certification for back-end developers, the Back-End Developer ABAP Cloud, is getting a major upgrade. At the same time, the SAP Community developer challenge for ABAP is going live, making this the perfect moment to sharpen your skills and validate your expertise.

 

A Reimagined Certification Experience

The ABAP Cloud certification exam has been redesigned. Moving beyond traditional question-based formats, the new exam introduces a performance-based, open-book experience.

Learners now work directly in authentic SAP systems, navigating AI-supported, real-world scenarios that reflect actual project work. The focus is no longer on memorization; it’s about demonstrating practical skills that matter on the job.

What does it mean for you?

If you’ve been preparing for certification through the SAP Learning site, you’re already familiar with hands-on exercises and scenario-based tasks embedded in the learning journeys. The new exam format simply extends that approach.

From the moment you begin your certification, the experience feels familiar because it mirrors how you’ve been learning all along.

Every practical exam, whether system-based or scenario-driven reflects the reality of SAP projects:

  • Timeboxed tasks performed in live SAP systems
  • Realistic customer and project scenarios
  • AI-supported simulations
  • Open-book access to resources such as SAP Help Portal, SAP Learning, SAP Community, and SAP Joule for Consultants (where applicable)
  • A clear emphasis on proving your ability to do the work

This shift represents something bigger: trust. Trust in learners who invest in building real skills and trust from employers who rely on SAP-certified professionals to deliver value from day one.

 

How to get certified as Backend Developer for ABAP Cloud

Earning your certification is a structured journey. Whether you are new to ABAP or looking to formalize years of experience, the following resources will help you build a strong foundation and deepen your expertise.

 

Recommended Courses to Build and Deepen Your Skills

Start with the fundamentals and progressively expand your knowledge:

  • Course: Learning Basic ABAP Programming
  • Course: Deepening Your ABAP Programming Knowledge
  • Course: Building Data Models with the ABAP Dictionary and ABAP Core Data Services
  • Course: Practicing Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Certification: Backend Developer - ABAP Cloud

 

Together, these courses prepare you not just to pass the exam but to succeed in real-world ABAP Cloud development. Learn more about the exam details in this community blog by @JenniferHauck.

 

Connect Live with SAP Experts and Strengthen Your Exam Readiness

Learning doesn’t have to be a solo experience. If you’re looking for direct interaction, practical tips, and expert guidance, join the upcoming live sessions available through your SAP Learning Hub subscription:

  • Getting Started with ABAP Cloud Development
  • Get Certified: SAP Certified – Back-End Developer ABAP Cloud
  • Stay Certified: Back-End Developer – ABAP Cloud

These sessions offer the opportunity to clarify questions, reinforce key concepts, and gain confidence before taking the exam.

 

Join the ABAP Developer Community to Learn, Share, and Grow

Certification is just one milestone in your journey. Continuous learning happens through collaboration.

Visit the ABAP developer forum here on the SAP Community to:

  • Read expert blogs
  • Ask technical questions
  • Share your own insights
  • Connect with developers worldwide

Being part of the community ensures you stay current and continue growing long after certification.

 

Experience Hands-On Learning at SAP CodeJam Events Near You

If you prefer immersive, in-person learning, don’t miss the upcoming SAP CodeJam events. These sessions are designed to provide practical, guided experience with real SAP technologies in a collaborative environment.

Upcoming sessions include:

  • April 7: ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (São Paulo, Brazil)
  • April 8: ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Londrina, Brazil)
  • April 23: ABAP Cloud and the RESTful Application Programming Model (Warsaw, Poland)

 

These events are an excellent opportunity to deepen your understanding, expand your network, and gain hands-on practice. Be sure to explore the full event calendar regularly, as new sessions are added on an ongoing basis.

 

Continue Your Learning with On-Demand Developer Content

Can’t attend live? Explore past Devtoberfest sessions, Tech Bytes, SAP CodeTalks, and more on the official SAP Developers YouTube channel

On-demand content allows you to learn at your own pace while staying connected to the latest innovations in ABAP and SAP development.

March is the perfect time to invest in your ABAP Cloud journey. With a certification experience designed to reflect real project work, you’re not just passing an exam, you’re demonstrating that you’re ready to build, extend, and innovate in the SAP ecosystem.

entry.title: Modelling IBP-to-IBP Integration Flows with SAP Integration Suite – Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello Community,

 

Introduction

Over the past years, CI-DS has been widely used to model integration interfaces with SAP IBP as both source and target system with or without an intermediate step via storing data in a file on server, covering use cases such as:

  • Housecleaning – Marking obsolete master data to alter clean within IBP. While S/4HANA's integration add-on can cleanse its own staging tables, this doesn't cascade automatically to IBP.
  • Rule-based attribute value definition – The Manage Rules for Master Data Maintenance app in IBP has limitations, for complex cases the integration framework is usually the way to go.
  • Data copies with transformations – Handling cases where the standard IBP Copy operator falls short: copying a key figure with a period offset, duplicating data from one planning object to multiple target planning objects, etc.
  • Version and planning area fan-out – Sending master data or key figures from one version (or planning area) to multiple versions (or planning areas), including data transformations.
  • Complex key figure calculations not yet simplified by the IBP configuration layer.
  • Master data attributes as KF reloads – Initializing time buckets at the end of the rolling time horizon.

Since IBP release 2508, the Harmonised Planning Area (HPA) has introduced a unified structure supporting both tactical and operational planning, combining time-series and order-based data. Alongside this major innovation, the integration layer is also evolving: as highlighted in a previous blog entry, the integration framework for IBP is shifting toward a combination of RTI and CI (the Cloud Integration capability of the Integration Suite service on BTP).

This blog entry continues the series on highlighting how to model IBP-to-IBP integration flows using Cloud Integration.

 

How to Model IBP-to-IBP Flows in Cloud Integration

Integration Suite offers the flexibility to cover these scenarios — and many others — within a single, configurable integration flow.

The starting point is the "SAP IBP – Reusable Integration Flows Examples" package. This package provides two sets of grouped reusable iFlows : first to read and extract data from a source IBP destination, another to write data to the to the same or a different IBP destination. Before building your own flows, I strongly recommend consulting the Integration guide for SAP IBP reusable integration flows examples.

One way to model this end to end is shown below as an overview:

 

📥 Step 1 – Read Data from IBP

To read data from IBP, call the following reusable iFlows in sequence, using RequestReply building blocks with a ProcessDirect adapter:

#

iFlow

Purpose

1

SAP_IBP_Read_-_Initialize

Opens a server-side read session on the IBP OData service, allocating resources to hold query context, pagination state, and result set.

2

SAP_IBP_Read_-_Fetch_Data

Fetches data pages from IBP's OData service, handling pagination automatically until all records have been extracted.

3

SAP_IBP_Read_-_Close

Closes the read session opened by Initialize. You can call this at the end of your flow — including after the write steps — to release server resources.

⚠️ Note: the Close iFlow is intentionally called last, after data transformation and all write steps have completed.

 

🔄 Step 2 – Transform Data

Once the XML payload has been fetched from IBP, you can apply transformations using the Message Mapping building block. This is where IBP-to-IBP flows deliver real value: transforming or adjusting data in ways that would be difficult or impossible to achieve directly within IBP.

Example: Housecleaning via a Deletion Indicator

Consider a scenario where S/4HANA regularly sends products to IBP based on an integration add-on filter (e.g., MRP type = X0). If a product is later made non-IBP-relevant, the add-on can stop sending it — but because data is loaded in insert/update mode, the product record remains in IBP master data.

A clean solution: use a timestamp attribute that tracks the last update date for each master data record. In your Message Mapping, you can evaluate this date and — if the record hasn't been updated since yesterday — mark it with an X in a Deletion Indicator attribute. The logic relies on the fact that master data is loaded daily: a record not updated today was not sent by S/4HANA and is therefore no longer integration-relevant.

Here's how to build this mapping once you've added it in your flow:

Load your source and target XSD schemas (an example is attached to this blog entry — rename the file extension from .xml to .xsd). You can use two different schema files for source and target, adapting each to include only the fields you're reading from or writing back to IBP. An XML editor like Notepad++ works well for these adjustments.

 

Once you've loaded both schemas, you can use the drag and drop method to link your attributes together 1:1.
For our example, focusing on the deletion indicator mechanism we will link the changed on date attribute (defined as timestamp in IBP) with the Product Deletion Indicator attribute (defined as nvarchar in IBP).

In the Mapping Expression panel, chain the following functions:

  1. substring – Trim the timestamp to YYYY-MM-DD format (10 characters)
  2. compareDates – Compare the result against currentDate, specifying the expected date format for both inputs
  3. equals(number) – Check the output of compareDates:
    • 0 → dates are equal (record updated today)
    • -1 → changed-on date is earlier than today
    • 1 → changed-on date is later than today
  4. If – If the result is -1, set the Deletion Indicator to X; otherwise leave it blank

 

🔧 Combining the Transformed Payload with Write Parameters

After the Message Mapping, your message carries transformed IBP master data. Before calling the write iFlows, you need to merge this payload with the 'Write' configuration parameters (batch name, IBP destination, master data type, etc.) passed via a Content Modifier step.

To do this cleanly:

   Filter the Message

Add a Filter building block immediately after the Message Mapping step.

  • Set the XPath expression to: //item

This strips the outer XML hierarchy (header node, <IBPReadMasterDataPRODUCT> wrapper) and retains only the <item> nodes carrying the actual master data values.

sidenote : to see this payload info above you need to enable Trace logs and deploy your whole iflow. see here how to enable these detailed logs.

   Save the Payload as an Exchange Property

Add a Content Modifier step. Create an exchange property with:

Field

Value

Name

payload (or any name you prefer)

Source Type

Expression

Source Value

${body}

Data Type

java.lang.String

Exchange properties persist throughout the entire flow and can be referenced at any later stage — this is the key to combining the two messages.

 

📤 Step 3 – Write Data to IBP

With the transformed data saved as an exchange property, you're ready to write back to IBP. Call the following reusable iFlows in sequence, again via RequestReply + ProcessDirect, as highlighted in the Integration guide for SAP IBP reusable integration flows examples.

#

iFlow

Purpose

1

SAP_IBP_Write_-_Create_Batches

Creates a batch header (name, ID, operation type: insert/update, replace, or delete) with no data yet. The batch is already visible in IBP's Data Integration app at this point.

2

SAP_IBP_Write_-_Post_Data

Creates batch items and loads master data or key figure records to the IBP staging tables.

3

SAP_IBP_Write_-_Process_Posted_Data

Triggers IBP's post-processing step to move data from staging tables to application tables, making it visible in planning.

You need two Content Modifier steps to supply the required parameters:

Before Create_Batches: Provide the batch key, batch name, command, and IBP destination. This step is already mentioned above as it also references your saved exchange property to carry the master data payload forward.

Before Post_Data: Provide the batch ID, master data type/prefix, and selected attributes. To inject the previously saved payload into the message body, simply reference the exchange property directly: ${property.payload}

💡 Flexibility at the Write Step

Because the IBP destination is a configurable parameter in the Content Modifier, it's straightforward to:

  • Send data to a different IBP tenant (e.g., read from Production, write to Test)
  • Send data to a different version, using the PlanningAreaVersion parameter (for master data types that support version-specific MD)
  • Send data to a different master data type (e.g., from pre-prod Product MD to quality Product MD) by adjusting the MasterDataPrefix / MasterDataType parameters

 

🔒 Step 4 – Close the Read Session

Always end your flow by calling SAP_IBP_Read_-_Close. This releases the server-side read session opened by Initialize and frees up resources on the IBP backend.

 

Summary

Initialize → Fetch_Data → [Message Mapping + Filter + Save Property] → Create_Batches → Post_Data → Process_Posted_Data → Close

This pattern gives you a single, configurable integration flow that can cover a wide range of IBP-to-IBP scenarios — housecleaning, data copies, version fan-out, cross-tenant refreshes — simply by adjusting parameters in your Content Modifier steps and adapting the Message Mapping logic.

I hope this walkthrough gives you a solid foundation for building your own IBP-to-IBP flows with Cloud Integration. Feel free to share questions or your own variations in the comments below!

entry.title: New course: Positioning SAP B2B Self Service Portal
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce a brand-new learning opportunity for sales and pre-sales professionals:

Positioning SAP B2B Self Service Portal 

Why SAP B2B Self Service Portal?

Business buyers expect consumer‑grade, 24/7 self‑service. The SAP B2B Self‑Service Portal helps companies meet those expectations while reducing the cost to serve and accelerating revenue. 

What Will You Learn?

This focused course goes beyond product features. After completing this course learners will be able to:

  • Understand the core capabilities and user experience of the SAP B2B Self‑Service Portal.
  • See how end‑to‑end order information is surfaced and how it improves transparency and response times.
  • Identify key business value drivers and articulate benefits such as reduced cost‑to‑serve, higher digital adoption, and revenue impact.
  • Leverage real‑world customer success stories to handle objections and inspire adoption.

Who Should Enroll?

  • Sales consultants working with SAP customers or prospects
  • Pre-sales consultants looking to stand out with specialized knowledge
  • Anyone eager to understand how SAP B2B Self Service Portal drives smarter business operations

Learn Today!

Ready to master SAP OMS positioning? Start learning now and take the first step toward equipping yourself with the knowledge and strategies you need to confidently position SAP B2B Self Service Portal.

 

We hope you enjoy this course and encourage you to share your valuable feedback with us. Thank you!

entry.title: IBP Integration with SAP Integration Suite – Your One-Stop Reference
entry.content:
👋 Welcome

This blog entry is a living index of resources covering SAP IBP integration using SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration). Whether you are just getting started or looking for guidance on a specific scenario, this is the place to find the right articles.

The goal of this entry is simple: as the series grows — with new posts from myself and from fellow community contributors — this page will be updated to reflect the latest content, so you always have a single reference point.

💡 Bookmark this page and check back regularly. It will be updated as new blog entries are published.

 

📚 Blog Series Index

 

🔷 Foundation & Setup

These entries cover the fundamentals: understanding why Cloud Integration is relevant for IBP and how to get your environment configured.

Blog Entry Description
Enhancing SAP IBP Integration – Evolution and InnovationBroader perspective on the evolution of the IBP integration landscape, covering both current capabilities and the direction of innovation. A good read for understanding the strategic context.
SAP IBP – New Integration Option: Introducing SAP Cloud Integration from BTPOverview of Cloud Integration as a new integration option for IBP. The starting point for the series — explains the shift in integration strategy and what CI brings to the table.

🔷 S/4HANA (private cloud or on-premise) to IBP Integration

Hands-on guidance for creating integration flows based on SAP delivered reusable content, focusing on S/4HANA private/on-prem as source or target.

Blog Entry Description
Configuring SAP Cloud Integration for an IBP-to-S/4HANA On-Premise (Private Cloud) scenarioStep-by-step configuration guide for connecting Cloud Integration to an S/4HANA on-premise or private cloud system from IBP. Covers connectivity setup, credentials, and key configuration parameters.
Creating Your First iFlow in Cloud Integration for an IBP / S/4HANA On-Prem scenarioA practical walkthrough for building your first integration flow between IBP and S/4HANA on-premise, using the reusable iFlow building blocks available in the IBP package.
IBP Integration Using SAP Cloud Integration – Fetch Product MD from Add-OnPractical guide to fetching Product master data from the S/4HANA integration add-on and loading it into IBP via Cloud Integration.

🔷 IBP-to-IBP Integration

Scenarios where IBP is both source and target — for data copies, transformations, housecleaning, and more.

Blog Entry Description
Modelling IBP-to-IBP Integration Flows with Cloud IntegrationHow to model a full IBP-to-IBP flow using the reusable iFlows: reading data with Initialize / Fetch / Close, transforming it via Message Mapping (including a housecleaning example), and writing it back using Create Batches / Post Data / Process Posted Data.

🔷 File-Based Integration

Covers integration scenarios involving file exchange via SFTP.

Blog Entry Description
IBP File-Based Integration with SFTP Server via Integration Suite – Cloud IntegrationHow to implement file-based integration for IBP using an SFTP server and Cloud Integration, including flow design, adapter configuration, and file handling patterns.

 

Want to Contribute?

If you have written or are planning a blog entry on IBP integration with Integration Suite, feel free to leave a comment below with your link and a short description. I will review it and add it to the index.

SAP Integrated Business Planning Cloud Integration SAP BTP ABAP environment SAP S/4HANA SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Missing Shared Dimensions in XML Export from HANA Studio
entry.content:
SAP HANA studio (version  2.3.65)

Hi Everyone,

I’m trying to generate a complete XML dump of my SAP HANA views (Aanalytic view)  for documentation and migration purposes.

Here’s my scenario:

I have a Data Foundation with several shared dimensions coming through joins.These shared dimensions are also present in the Semantics layer and used in downstream views.However, when I export the objects to XML from HANA Studio, I cannot find some of these shared dimension fields in the XML dump.

It looks like the fields exist and are being used downstream, but they are missing from the exported XML.

Has anyone faced this issue before?
Is there a reliable way to include all shared dimensions (even those coming through joins and used in downstream views) in the XML export?

Any tips, workarounds, or settings I might be missing would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Time Account Type Validity in Successfactors
entry.content:
Hi LA_01
1. Create a Time Account first  - Provide External Name, workflow Configurations, Unit, Country, Permitted Fractions for Unit Day; 

2. Go ahead and create Take Rules, like backdate should allowed, negative should be allowed or not.

3. Time Account Type - Same External Name, Unit : Days, Account Creation Type: Recurring, Automatic Account Creation - No Automation, Accrual Creation offset -1, Hire Rule, Termination Rule, Accrual Rule (Ensure you config it properly).

4. While creating a  Leave Account : Account Validity & Bookable Period for 1st Year be from 1-Apr-xx to 31-Dec-xx; you can do a manual PEP on 31-Dec or 1-Jan

5. While you create a Next Year Leave Account : Account Validity & Bookable Period be 1-Jan-xx to 31-Dec-xx, Set PEP Automatic from this year onwards.

Regards
Ajay Kulkarni

entry.title: 🎙️ Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curitiba
entry.content:
Comunidade não é só evento.
É troca, conexão e pessoas que constroem juntas.


Pensando nisso, iniciamos a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, um espaço para dar voz às pessoas que ajudam a fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade.


Mais do que falar sobre tecnologia, queremos compartilhar histórias reais, trajetórias, desafios e conexões que fazem a comunidade acontecer.
💛Valorizar quem constrói a comunidade
🔗Fortalecer conexões entre profissionais SAP
🚀Inspirar novas pessoas a entrarem no ecossistema
🌱Mostrar que comunidade é colaboração, não competição
👩‍💻Dar visibilidade a talentos locais


📌Este é o post de referência da série.

Aqui você encontra o template para quem for convidado(a) a participar.

📌COMO PARTICIPAR DA SÉRIE
Se você for convidado(a), siga este formato no seu post:


1️⃣Título do post
Comece seu post com o título:
Somos SAP Community Curitiba – [Seu nome]


2️⃣Comece citando este post de referência
Inclua o link deste post no início do seu texto.
Exemplo:
Esta publicação faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba.
O template da série está neste post de referência: [link deste post]


3️⃣Mencione quem te convidou
Comece dizendo quem te convidou para a série e mencione o post da pessoa.
Exemplo:
Fui convidado(a) por [@nome da pessoa] para participar da série
Somos SAP Community Curitiba.
Você pode conferir o post dela/dele aqui: [link do post]


4️⃣Responda às 3 perguntas

  • Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
  • O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
  • Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?


5️⃣Continue a corrente
Convide no mínimo 1 pessoa da comunidade para continuar a série.
Se quiser, você pode convidar mais pessoas também.
Exemplo:
Passando a corrente 🚀
Agora eu convido [@nome da pessoa] a contar um pouco da sua história e da sua conexão com a SAP Community Curitiba.


Minha pergunta para você é:
[Pergunta livre]


6️⃣Opcional
Você também pode incluir no seu post:
📷Uma foto
🔗Suas redes sociais


💡Dicas

  • Ao convidar alguém, lembre-se de avisar a pessoa para que ela saiba que foi convidada a continuar a série.
  • Fique à vontade para compartilhar o link do seu post também no LinkedIn, usando as hashtags da série para ajudar a ampliar a visibilidade da comunidade.

 

Hashtags da série
#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity


Se você faz parte da SAP Community Curitiba, essa corrente também é sua.

Vamos construir essa história juntos.


🚀Em breve publicarei o primeiro post iniciando essa série.


Comunidade se constrói com pessoas.
E agora queremos ouvir as histórias de quem faz a SAP Community Curitiba acontecer.
Se você faz parte da comunidade e gostaria de participar da série, deixe seu nome nos comentários deste post.
Será um prazer ampliar ainda mais essa corrente.

🔗Conecte-se com a SAP Community Curitiba
Quer acompanhar as novidades da comunidade ou participar das próximas iniciativas?
Siga nossos canais:
💼LinkedIn
📸Instagram
💬Grupo WhatsApp da comunidade


Assim você fica por dentro de eventos, encontros, conteúdos e iniciativas da SAP Community Curitiba.

entry.title: 9 ways to create Interface Fact Sheets in SAP LeanIX
entry.content:
Interface Fact Sheets in LeanIX can explain the data flow between two applications on a logical and on a technical level. A logical interface would be "Replicate BusinessPartner Create from SAP S/4HANA Finance to SalesForce". A technical interface might be one specific integration component (e.g. Integration Flow on SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration or the API Proxy in front of SalesForce on API Management) which can be only a part of the logical interface.

 

The criteria to differentiate the integration options is 

  • level of automation
  • granularity (logical and/or technical interface)
  • which data sources are supported

Here´s the list:

 

Option

Automation & Granularity

Supported Data Sources

1. Manual Entry 

manual (logical)

n/a – no integration used

2. Bulk Import

semi-automated (technical)

n/a – no integration used

3. GraphQL API

automated (logical/technical)

MAKE: whatever you program

4. LeanIX Adapter

automated (logical/technical)

MAKE: whatever you program

5. Standard Content: NTT Data

automated (logical only)

SAP IS: Integration Assessment

6. Standard Content: RealCoreautomated (technical only)SAP IS: Cloud Integration
SAP IS: API Management
SAP Process Orchestration
7. SAP Cloud ALMsemi-automated (technical only)Monitored SAP-Systems

8. LeanIX MCP Server

automated (logical/technical)

Create not possible yet, potentially various data sources in future

9. WHINT Integration Cockpit

automated (logical & technical)

Multiple: SAP and Non-SAP Integration Layer Components

You can find a more detailed explanation in the Integration Excellence blog: https://www.integration-excellence.com/how-to-feed-sap-leanix-with-interface-fact-sheets/

entry.title: Technical validation request: multiple copies of the same legacy system integrating simultaneously
entry.content:
Technical validation request: multiple copies of the same legacy system integrating simultaneously to S/4HANA (RISE) QAS, end-to-end SD flow (synchronous)

I’m designing an integration solution for a customer and would like your help validating technical feasibility — both the overall architecture and the specific integrations — before proceeding.

Scenario

  • Customer on SAP RISE with S/4HANA (QAS).
  • Up to 10 copies of the same legacy system (Instance 1 to Instance 10) integrating simultaneously against the same QAS environment.
  • Full Sales flow, synchronous: ATP Check → Sales Order → Delivery → PGI → Billing → Status Return.

Proposed architecture (summary)

  • Central hub on SAP BTP using SAP Integration Suite (iFlows per process), identifying each legacy copy via headers:
    • X-Sender-System (business logical system)
    • X-Sender-Instance (copy index: 1..10)
  • SAP API Management for authentication, rate limiting, and distinct credentials per instance.
  • On S/4HANA QAS:
    • Communication System and Communication Arrangement per instance (up to 10 in parallel).
    • Use standard S/4 OData APIs for the flow: API_ATP_CHECK, API_SALES_ORDER_SRV, API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV, API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV.
    • SAP Application Interface Framework (AIF) for functional monitoring and traceability, using CorrelationID + SenderInstance as key fields (to isolate reprocessing per instance).

Note: The proposal is fully synchronous end-to-end for testing; no messaging/events here.

Example flow (synchronous, high level)

  1. Legacy Instance n calls ATP; the hub validates headers/credentials, forwards to S/4, and returns the response.
  2. Sales Order creation via API_SALES_ORDER_SRV; confirmation returns synchronously to the same instance.
  3. Delivery creation and PGI via logistics APIs; confirmations return to the caller.
  4. Billing via API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV; document number is returned to the corresponding instance.
  5. The entire cycle is traceable in AIF and Integration Suite monitoring by instance (SenderInstance) and CorrelationID.

Questions for the community

  1. Feasibility on RISE (Cloud Private Edition):
    • Are there any platform, tenant, or contractual constraints in RISE that would prevent this hub pattern with multiple instances of the same legacy calling QAS in parallel?
  2. Coverage of S/4HANA OData APIs (RISE):
    • Do the listed OData APIs (ATP, Sales Order, Outbound Delivery, Billing) support the full, 100% synchronous flow in this context?
    • Are there process steps that typically require alternatives (e.g., BAPI/IDoc) even in synchronous scenarios?
  3. Scale of Communication Systems/Arrangements:
    • Is it feasible to have up to 10 Communication Systems/Arrangements active for the same SD scenario in QAS simultaneously, one per legacy instance, without practical limitations?
  4. Isolation in AIF:
    • Does AIF support isolating reprocessing per instance (via key fields SenderInstance + CorrelationID), ensuring reprocessing for one instance does not affect others running in parallel?
  5. iFlow parameterization:
    • Do iFlows with Externalized Parameters per instance (endpoints, credentials, toggles) work on the RISE BTP tenant as designed? Any best practices to separate 10 instances without “exploding” the number of artifacts?

Any additional guidance is welcome

  • Naming best practices (Communication System/Arrangement per instance).
  • Practical throughput/concurrency limits in QAS for parallel SD testing.
  • Lessons learned for 100% synchronous SD flows (especially for Delivery/PGI/Billing via OData).

entry.title: Installing and extending an ABAP MCP Server by vibe-coding with Claude Code and Google Antigravity
entry.content:
On one rainy morning I mentioned to my Google Antigravity Agent (Claude Opus 4.6 Thinking):

"I hear people are experimenting with connecting to SAP Systems via MCP. They use ABAP Development Tools API as a way in. Could you help me set up something like that for my ABAP Trial system?"

It searched the Web and found Mario Andreschak's MCP-ABAP-ADT solution on GitHub: https://github.com/mario-andreschak/mcp-abap-adt. Then it came up with the following implementation plan and asked a few questions:

I approved the plan and answered the questions. After a while, it was done cloning Mario's Repository and installing:

I updated the Password and Client in the *.env file and switched to Claude Code, who (Don't anthropomorphise computers, they hate it!) can act as an MCP Client. Claude Code was able to connect to my ABAP Trial using Mario's MCP Server.

Not having studied Mario's documentation, I didn't realise his MCP Server was for reading only. I asked Claude Code to add a comment line to an ABAP Program, just as a test. It said it couldn't do it, on account of there being no WRITE, and offered to extend the MCP Server with WRITE capability. I said yes.

It didn't take long for Claude to do this, but I hit my rate limit and had to wait until the afternoon (Claude Code sessions are 5-hour windows with a limit on tokens. In addition, there are weekly limits).

There was a bit of a kerfuffle regarding the locking, but Claude Code managed to sort it all out by himself.

And sure enough, the test ABAP Program was updated with a comment. Actually, two comments - one was there from before, when Claude was testing the API via CURL.

As I was again nearing my Claude Code usage limits, I switched back to Google Antigravity. I asked the agent there to add even more WRITE tools: For Classes and Interfaces, plus a tool to create Programs from scratch. My request was dispatched rather swiftly.

I asked the Agent what other functionalities could he add to the MCP Server. As one would expect, he listed some of the many that are supported by the ADT API:

All we have to do is ask 😊

 

P.S.:

- The SAP System was ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023,

- I used a Mac,

- Claude Code and Google Gemini paid plans. Not any fancy "Max" plans, just the regular 20-EUR-per-month ones.
